title: Luce County, Michigan

infobox.value: ([/luːs/](/wiki/Help:IPA/English "Help:IPA/English") [*LOOS*](/wiki/Help:Pronunciation_respelling_key "Help:Pronunciation respelling key")) is a [county](/wiki/County_(United_States) "County (United States)") located in the [Upper Peninsula](/wiki/Upper_Peninsula "Upper Peninsula") in the [U.S. state](/wiki/U.S._state "U.S. state") of [Michigan](/wiki/Michigan "Michigan"). As of the [2020 census](/wiki/2020_United_States_census "2020 United States census"), the population was 5,339,[[2]](#cite_note-QF-2) making it the second-least populous county in Michigan (behind [Keweenaw County](/wiki/Keweenaw_County,_Michigan "Keweenaw County, Michigan")). The [county seat](/wiki/County_seat "County seat") is [Newberry](/wiki/Newberry,_Michigan "Newberry, Michigan"), Luce County's only incorporated community.[[3]](#cite_note-GR6-3) The county was set off and organized in 1887 and named after former [Michigan Governor](/wiki/Michigan_Governor "Michigan Governor") [Cyrus G. Luce](/wiki/Cyrus_G._Luce "Cyrus G. Luce").[[1]](#cite_note-clarke-1) In 2002, Newberry was designated as the [moose](/wiki/Moose "Moose") capital of Michigan by the state legislature.[[4]](#cite_note-Newberry_Tourism-4) Luce County is served by The Newberry News, a weekly community newspaper that began in 1886.

**Luce County** ([/luːs/](/wiki/Help:IPA/English "Help:IPA/English") [*LOOS*](/wiki/Help:Pronunciation_respelling_key "Help:Pronunciation respelling key")) is a [county](/wiki/County_(United_States) "County (United States)") located in the [Upper Peninsula](/wiki/Upper_Peninsula "Upper Peninsula") in the [U.S. state](/wiki/U.S._state "U.S. state") of [Michigan](/wiki/Michigan "Michigan"). As of the [2020 census](/wiki/2020_United_States_census "2020 United States census"), the population was 5,339,[[2]](#cite_note-QF-2) making it the second-least populous county in Michigan (behind [Keweenaw County](/wiki/Keweenaw_County,_Michigan "Keweenaw County, Michigan")). The [county seat](/wiki/County_seat "County seat") is [Newberry](/wiki/Newberry,_Michigan "Newberry, Michigan"), Luce County's only incorporated community.[[3]](#cite_note-GR6-3) The county was set off and organized in 1887 and named after former [Michigan Governor](/wiki/Michigan_Governor "Michigan Governor") [Cyrus G. Luce](/wiki/Cyrus_G._Luce "Cyrus G. Luce").[[1]](#cite_note-clarke-1) In 2002, Newberry was designated as the [moose](/wiki/Moose "Moose") capital of Michigan by the state legislature.[[4]](#cite_note-Newberry_Tourism-4) Luce County is served by The Newberry News, a weekly community newspaper that began in 1886.

## Geography

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Luce_County,_Michigan&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Geography")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/3/33/UpperTahquamenonFalls2.jpg/250px-UpperTahquamenonFalls2.jpg)](/wiki/File:UpperTahquamenonFalls2.jpg)

[Upper Tahquamenon Falls](/wiki/Tahquamenon_Falls "Tahquamenon Falls")

According to the [U.S. Census Bureau](/wiki/U.S._Census_Bureau "U.S. Census Bureau"), the county has a total area of 1,912 sq mi (4,950 km2), of which 899 sq mi (2,330 km2) is land and 1,013 sq mi (2,620 km2) (53%) is water.[[5]](#cite_note-GR1-5) Luce County is part of the [Upper Peninsula](/wiki/Upper_Peninsula "Upper Peninsula") of [Michigan](/wiki/Michigan "Michigan"). It has a northern border with [Canada](/wiki/Canada "Canada") across [Lake Superior](/wiki/Lake_Superior "Lake Superior").

[McMillan Township](/wiki/McMillan_Township,_Luce_County,_Michigan "McMillan Township, Luce County, Michigan"), the largest [municipality](/wiki/Municipality "Municipality") in Michigan by land area (at 588.78 square miles (1,524.9 km2) of total land area), is part of Luce County.

### Major highways

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Luce_County,_Michigan&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Major highways")]

* ![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/f/fd/M-28.svg/40px-M-28.svg.png) [M-28](/wiki/M-28_(Michigan_highway) "M-28 (Michigan highway)") is Michigan's longest [state trunkline highway](/wiki/List_of_state_trunkline_highways_in_Michigan "List of state trunkline highways in Michigan"). An east–west route, M-28 can be used to access [Sault Ste. Marie](/wiki/Sault_Ste._Marie,_Michigan "Sault Ste. Marie, Michigan") to the east, and [Munising](/wiki/Munising,_Michigan "Munising, Michigan") and [Marquette](/wiki/Marquette,_Michigan "Marquette, Michigan") to the west.
* ![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/b/bf/M-117_rectangle.svg/40px-M-117_rectangle.svg.png) [M-117](/wiki/M-117_(Michigan_highway) "M-117 (Michigan highway)") is a north–south connector between M-28 west of Newberry to [US 2](/wiki/U.S._Route_2_in_Michigan "U.S. Route 2 in Michigan") in [Mackinac County](/wiki/Mackinac_County,_Michigan "Mackinac County, Michigan").
* ![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/9/96/M-123_rectangle.svg/40px-M-123_rectangle.svg.png) [M-123](/wiki/M-123_(Michigan_highway) "M-123 (Michigan highway)") is a u-shaped highway, beginning at M-28 south of Newberry. ### 2020 census

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Luce_County,_Michigan&action=edit&section=11 "Edit section: 2020 census")]

As of the [2020 census](/wiki/2020_United_States_census "2020 United States census"), the county had a population of 5,339. The median age was 49.1 years. 20.1% of residents were under the age of 18 and 25.9% of residents were 65 years of age or older. For every 100 females there were 102.0 males, and for every 100 females age 18 and over there were 99.3 males age 18 and over.[[11]](#cite_note-Census2020DP-11)

The racial makeup of the county was 84.9% [White](/wiki/White_Americans "White Americans"), 0.2% [Black or African American](/wiki/African_Americans "African Americans"), 6.9% [American Indian and Alaska Native](/wiki/Native_Americans_in_the_United_States "Native Americans in the United States"), <0.1% [Asian](/wiki/Asian_Americans "Asian Americans"), <0.1% [Native Hawaiian](/wiki/Native_Hawaiians "Native Hawaiians") and [Pacific Islander](/wiki/Pacific_Islander "Pacific Islander"), 0.3% from some other race, and 7.6% from [two or more races](/wiki/Multiracial_Americans "Multiracial Americans"). [Hispanic or Latino](/wiki/Hispanic_and_Latino_Americans "Hispanic and Latino Americans") residents of any race comprised 1.4% of the population.[[12]](#cite_note-Census2020PL-12)

<0.1% of residents lived in urban areas, while 100.0% lived in rural areas.[[13]](#cite_note-Census2020DHC-13)

There were 2,344 households in the county, of which 22.9% had children under the age of 18 living in them. Of all households, 46.5% were married-couple households, 21.3% were households with a male householder and no spouse or partner present, and 23.5% were households with a female householder and no spouse or partner present. About 31.1% of all households were made up of individuals and 16.1% had someone living alone who was 65 years of age or older.[[11]](#cite_note-Census2020DP-11)

There were 4,065 housing units, of which 42.3% were vacant. Among occupied housing units, 79.0% were owner-occupied and 21.0% were renter-occupied. The homeowner vacancy rate was 3.0% and the rental vacancy rate was 14.7%.[[11]](#cite_note-Census2020DP-11)

### 2010 census

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Luce_County,_Michigan&action=edit&section=12 "Edit section: 2010 census")]

The [2010 census](/wiki/2010_United_States_Census "2010 United States Census") indicated Luce County had a population of 6,631.[[14]](#cite_note-American_Fact_Finder,_Census_2010-14) This is a decrease of 393 people from the [2000 United States census](/wiki/2000_United_States_census "2000 United States census"). This is a -5.6% change in population. In 2010 there were 2,412 households and 1,542 families residing in the county. The [population density](/wiki/Population_density "Population density") was 7 people per square mile (2.7 people/km2). There were 4,343 housing units at an average density of 5 per square mile (1.9/km2).

There were 2,412 households, out of which 24.3% had children under the age of 18 living with them, 50.2% were [married couples](/wiki/Marriage "Marriage") living together, 9.3% had a female householder with no husband present, and 36.1% were non-families. 31.4% of all households were made up of individuals, and 13.8% had someone living alone who was 65 years of age or older. The average household size was 2.25 and the average family size was 2.77. In the county, the population was spread out, with 17.9% under the age of 18, 7.0% from 18 to 24, 27.5% from 25 to 44, 29.6% from 45 to 64, and 17.9% who were 65 years of age or older. The median age was 43 years. The population was 57.7% male and 42.3% female.

At the 2010 census, the racial makeup of the county was 80.4% [White](/wiki/White_American "White American"), 11.1% [Black or African American](/wiki/African_American "African American"), 5.0% [Native American](/wiki/Native_Americans_in_the_United_States "Native Americans in the United States"), 0.3% [Asian](/wiki/Asian_American "Asian American") and 3.1% [of two or more races](/wiki/Multiracial_American "Multiracial American"); of them, 1.2% were [Hispanic or Latino](/wiki/Hispanic_and_Latino_Americans "Hispanic and Latino Americans") (of any race). In 2010 among its population, 14.5% were of [German](/wiki/German_American "German American"), 7.6% [French](/wiki/French_American "French American"), [French Canadian](/wiki/French_Canadian_American "French Canadian American") or [Cajun](/wiki/Cajun "Cajun"), 7.4% [Irish](/wiki/Irish_American "Irish American"), 6.8% [English](/wiki/English_American "English American"), 6.3% [American](/wiki/American_ethnicity "American ethnicity") and 5.5% [Finnish](/wiki/Finnish_American "Finnish American") ancestry.[[15]](#cite_note-15)

In 2010, the median income for a household in the county was $42,083, and the median income for a family was $49,948. The [per capita income](/wiki/Per_capita_income "Per capita income") for the county was $18,294. About 12.6% of families and 16.3% of the population were below the [poverty line](/wiki/Poverty_line "Poverty line"), including 27.7% of those under age 18 and 12.5% of those age 65 or over.
